editorial.headline: Antonacci's Walk-Off Single Lifts White Sox Over Guardians
editorial.lead_angle:
Sam Antonacci sent a hard single back through the box, plating two runs in the bottom of the ninth to give the Chicago White Sox a chaotic 6-5 walk-off win against the Cleveland Guardians. The liner off reliever Cade Smith capped a back-and-forth final inning that saw both teams trade go-ahead blows. Chicago looked finished just moments earlier, but Antonacci’s clean hit sent the Rate Field crowd into a frenzy and handed the Guardians a brutal divisional loss.

The final frame was a study in reversals. Cleveland entered the ninth trailing by a run but surged ahead when pinch-hitter Kahlil Watson delivered a two-run single to give the Guardians a late lead. The visitors were three outs from victory. But the White Sox answered immediately against Smith, putting two runners on with one out to set the stage. Antonacci, who had driven in a run earlier, worked the count before finding a hole in the Cleveland infield for the game-winner.

Antonacci was the difference-maker all evening for Chicago, finishing 2-for-4 with three runs batted in. The victory went to Chris Murphy, who recorded the final out in the top of the ninth. More importantly, the win pulls the White Sox closer in the standings, thwarting Cleveland’s chance to create separation in the divisional race. For one night, the South Siders found an answer when they needed it most.
editorial.reason: Walk-offs are baseball's signature drama. The season's best finish this day.
